channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Road closed sign on Andover Roac, 8th March
item.description:
There is a Severn Trent road closed sign on Andover Road, close to junction with Park Place which is out of date (says 23-25 Feb)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25, Andover Road, Tivoli, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole just outside of Oldwell Cottage driveway, 7th March
item.description:
Just outside of Oldwell Cottage driveway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blacksmiths Lane, Leigh, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside Tesco, Queen's Road, Cheltenham, 7th March
item.description:
I would like an answer to the question: Why does GCC use such a poor specification for the repair of potholes and resurfacing of trenches? Other contractors use a far better spec which lasts. There was an enormous pothole in the bus layby outside Tesco in Queen’s Road. One Sunday a few years ago, Atkins came and repaired it and installed a gully. This never drained properly, so a gang dug trenches to the gully, which then worked. However, it was obvious the trench surfacing would not last, and sure enough another gang came and did repairs to one of the trenches (again, poor quality). That’s 3 visits so far to repair one problem, and potholes are again appearing in the trench surface. If you’d done the job properly, you could have been getting on with other jobs. It's the same with a pothole at the rear of the station, in the toucan crossing in Gloucester Road. The pothole was repaired in 2019, 2020 and 2021. It is holding so far, but won’t last many more years, whereas other non-GCC holes and trenches in the vicinity are holding up extremely well. So – Why does GCC use such a poor spec for its repairs?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massive potholes, 7th March
item.description:
These potholes have been there for over 2 weeks, and is just getting worse. Is impossible not to go thru them. This is an emergency as cars will be damaged.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Hayden Road, Hesters Way, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin outside wok wagon, 7th March
item.description:
Overflowing bin out side wok wagon
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 37, Old Cheltenham Road, Longlevens, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There are multiple potholes and areas of broken roadway. This area is extremely dangerous now for cyclists and it is only a matter of time (if it hasn’t happened already) that ther will be a collision as cars straddle the potholes down the centre of the road. This has been getting worse for months now and the council must accept liability for the repercussions when they inevitably come, 7th March
item.description:
The majority of the break ups and potholes are centered pretty much opposite the entrance to West Car Park

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Swindon Lane, Marle Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Police tape and sandbag need to be collected, 7th March
item.description:
Please ensure that your contractor doesn't forget these. Behind the tree with the police tape on it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 267a, London Road, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very poor road surface from racecourse to Tommy Taylors Lane, 7th March
item.description:
I reported this before and had an email to say it was fixed. I have just driven down the road and it is worse than before. Definitely not fixed.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 22, Swindon Lane, Marle Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A pothole in road problem, 7th March
item.description:
Large pothole. larger vthan dinnerplate. about 5 inches deep. More potholes, further down road where 2 lanes meet.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Volunteer litter pick bags ready for collection, 7th March
item.description:
Next to Innsworth Lane Sports Ground sign. The 'What 3 Words' location is: ///examiner.slopes.courage The ordnance survey map reference is: 51.883919,-2.213313 Council Litter Bin Ref: 0254

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Innsworth Lane, Longlevens, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Litter picking bags ready for collection

item.title: Two deep holes together, 7th March
item.description:
When they last fixed this problem a month or so ago, something was used which is now splashed all over my gates (114 Charlton lad) in black tar. It looks like the pothole has worn down to that layer and that part has now decorated my gates. Very very difficult to get off with powder coated aluminium. Also a damaging amount of grit always works its way loose and splatters my gates. All in all it’s causing damage. Please please can this be more a permanent fix with the right materials so it goes away forever. If It’s a temporary fix then it just opens up again and again

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 109, Charlton Lane, Naunton, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple potholes on Blacksmith`s Lane, 7th March
item.description:
Multiple large potholes and general road erosion outside Bishops Cottage, Blacksmith`s Lane. Difficult to drive on, and potential to damage vehicles. Road now open for Highways investigation following closure due to recent flooding.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blacksmith Lane, Leigh, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
